purpose: Authoritative source registry for knowledge taxonomies, reference typing, review status, and keyword enrichment.

sources.name: OECD Frascati Manual 2015 (FORD)
sources.recommendedUse: Seed the canonical research-field hierarchy for projects and references.
sources.recommendedScope: Seed a normalized FORD concept tree in local taxonomy tables.
notes: Treat FORD as canonical for research fields.
No separate official CSV or RDF table was verified; seed from the official manual.

sources.name: UNESCO ISCED-F 2013
sources.recommendedUse: Use only when program or study-field classification is needed alongside FORD.
sources.recommendedScope: Seed as an optional parallel taxonomy for academic programs and templates.
notes: The official site exposes document pages and downloads, but the PDF URL was not verified as stable enough for automated direct download.
Register the page as the durable reference and extract the table manually when seeding.

sources.name: UNESCO Thesaurus
sources.recommendedUse: Use for controlled keywords and multilingual subject normalization.
sources.recommendedScope: Import selectively; do not dump every concept into product UX before needed.
notes: This vocabulary conforms to ISO 25964.
Prefer this for controlled keywords, not as the only field-of-science hierarchy.

sources.name: COAR Resource Types
sources.recommendedUse: Seed specific scholarly resource types such as journal article, review article, master thesis, and doctoral thesis.
sources.recommendedScope: Use as the canonical specific resource type layer.
notes: Best source for specific scholarly document genres.
Use with DCMI Type for the broad type and DataCite for interoperability hints.

sources.name: COAR Version Types
sources.recommendedUse: Map preprint, accepted manuscript, version of record, corrected version, and related publication stages.
sources.recommendedScope: Seed the publication-stage layer for references when explicit evidence exists.
notes: Use this for publication stage, not for peer review status.
The vocabulary is derived from NISO JAV recommendations.

sources.name: DataCite Metadata Schema
sources.recommendedUse: Use as an interoperability reference for DOI-facing resource typing and export compatibility.
sources.recommendedScope: Reference-only in Release 0; do not treat it as the canonical scholarly-type hierarchy.
notes: Use DataCite to align resource-type naming at DOI boundaries.
Keep COAR as the main specific scholarly type vocabulary.

sources.name: NDLTD ETD-MS
sources.recommendedUse: Reference metadata profile for theses and dissertations.
sources.recommendedScope: Reference-only for thesis-oriented exports and mappings.
notes: Use to shape thesis metadata semantics such as degree details and thesis-specific fields.
The source was verified by web lookup, but direct download was not confirmed from the local shell.

sources.name: ANSI/NISO Peer Review Terminology
sources.recommendedUse: Reference vocabulary for peer review terminology and review-model dimensions.
sources.recommendedScope: Seed a small controlled local enum set and preserve evidence; do not infer peer review from type alone.
notes: Use for peer-review semantics only.
This should remain distinct from publication stage and indexing.

sources.name: OpenAlex Topics, Fields, Subfields, Keywords, and Work Types
sources.recommendedUse: Use for enrichment, candidate field assignment, keyword suggestions, and retrieval features.
sources.recommendedScope: Do not use as the sole canonical taxonomy; map to local canonical axes.
sources.licenseOrRights: OpenAlex open catalog and API terms
notes: Use OpenAlex as enrichment, not as the final canonical classification authority.
Avoid the experimental or deprecated text-aboutness endpoints for core production workflows.

sources.name: Planimetrix Structural and Seismic Engineering Taxonomy
sources.recommendedUse: Use for structural engineering, earthquake engineering, seismic protection systems, seismic isolation, and seismic isolator terminology not covered at sufficient granularity by FORD.
sources.recommendedScope: Seed as a small local extension taxonomy for structural and seismic research assistance.
sources.licenseOrRights: Local curated asset with external source provenance
notes: Curated from authoritative terminology used by FEMA, NIST, and ASCE.
Use this as a local extension, not as a replacement for FORD or COAR.

sources.name: Planimetrix Applied Research Domains Taxonomy
sources.recommendedUse: Use for the dominant applied research branches already present in local projects and references, including business, information systems, health, education, human talent, urban studies, and public management.
sources.recommendedScope: Seed as a local extension taxonomy for project routing, keyword mapping, and domain-aware retrieval.
sources.licenseOrRights: Local curated asset derived from current product data and domain normalization work
notes: Curated from dominant local project programs and current keyword distribution in the reference catalog.
Use this as a local applied-domain layer over FORD and COAR.
